title: 'No Kings' protests against Trump focus on war in Iran: 'The US is on the wrong side of history'
subhead: Millions turned out to protests across the US against the Trump administration, with the movement's epicenter located in the twin cities of Minneapolis-Saint Paul, after two Americans were killed by federal agents there in January.

title: Iranians live under constant anxiety: 'When I close my eyes, I wonder if I will see the next day'
Iran's Revolutionary Guard threatens US universities in the Middle East
War returns to southern Lebanon: 'Southern people have endured so many sacrifices'
subhead: Hassan Ayoub and Mohamed Fakih are two left-wing Shiite Lebanese men. Through their life stories, they go over the history of their homeland, a poor and long-marginalized region that has been scarred by repeated conflicts with Israel.

title: In Monaco, Pope Leo XIV delivers a message on wealth redistribution: 'Every good entrusted to us has a universal destination'
subhead: On his first trip to Europe, the American pontiff met the princely family and defended a faith that must combat inequality, though he struggled to break from Monaco's opulent traditions.

title: Substack, a digital refuge for writers, accelerates its growth in France
subhead: Since 2017, the US platform has enabled users to publish content as monetizable newsletters, winning over many journalists and writers. The company recently appointed a manager to boost its growth in France.

title: Escaping Putin's Russia: A father and daughter persecuted over a drawing, now in exile in France
subhead: Alexei Moskalyov and his daughter, Maria, are symbolic victims of the Kremlin's repression. The father was jailed after posting an anti-war drawing his teenage daughter made at school. They have just arrived in France and are under humanitarian protection.

In Nabatieh, southern Lebanon, 'no one ventures outside anymore apart from rescue teams'
subhead: Like 42 other rescue workers since the war began on March 2, 16-year-old Joude Souleiman was killed by an Israeli drone strike in Nabatieh, the second-largest Shiite city in southern Lebanon, where only a few hundred people out of 75,000 residents remain.

title: 'Trump would benefit from rereading Obama's 2015 speech on Iran'
subhead: The US president belatedly realized that the military option carried significant risks. He did not rely on his administration's recognized experts on Iran, notes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

title: Volodymyr Zelensky: 'The pressure on Russia is insufficient. Not only from the US, but also from Europe'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, the Ukrainian president expressed concern about the consequences of the war in the Middle East on the conflict between Kyiv and Moscow. Pressured by both the Americans and the Russians to give up the Donbas, he defended the region's strategic importance and warned Europeans of the urgent need to lift the Hungarian veto on the €90 billion loan approved in 2025.

title: Ukrainians' suffering must not be forgotten amid the war against Iran
